A method that is not declared with `this: void` may cause unintentional scoping of `this` when separated from its object.
Consider using an arrow function or explicitly `.bind()`ing the method to avoid calling the method with an unintended `this` value. 
If a function does not access `this`, it can be annotated with `this: void`.

messages.message:
A method that is not declared with `this: void` may cause unintentional scoping of `this` when separated from its object.
Consider using an arrow function or explicitly `.bind()`ing the method to avoid calling the method with an unintended `this` value. 
If a function does not access `this`, it can be annotated with `this: void`.

A method that is not declared with `this: void` may cause unintentional scoping of `this` when separated from its object.
Consider using an arrow function or explicitly `.bind()`ing the method to avoid calling the method with an unintended `this` value. 
If a function does not access `this`, it can be annotated with `this: void`.

Consider using an arrow function or explicitly `.bind()`ing the method to avoid calling the method with an unintended `this` value. 
If a function does not access `this`, it can be annotated with `this: void`.
Unsafe argument of type `any` assigned to a parameter of type `DataSourceOptions`.
Unexpected any. Specify a different type.

suggestions.desc: Use `unknown` instead, this will force you to explicitly, and safely assert the type is correct.
Use `never` instead, this is useful when instantiating generic type parameters that you don't need to know the type of.

messages.message: Use "@ts-expect-error" instead of "@ts-ignore", as "@ts-ignore" will do nothing if the following line is error-free.

suggestions.desc: Replace "@ts-ignore" with "@ts-expect-error".
